name: Sub-agent fleet — capability layer beneath Secretary's orchestrator
next_move: Stand up agents/ directory with one subfolder per sub-agent (each with agents.md declaring jurisdiction, inputs, outputs, failure modes, escalation rules). Ship a registry index and a delegation mechanism that returns typed results or typed abstention. First 3–5 sub-agents that earn their keep immediately: research/enrichment, message-drafting, red-team, voice-audit (enforces the Memory voice model), calendar/scheduling.
blockers: Location of the ~21 specialist sub-agent specs from earlier framework work — need to pull into agents/
Delegation return type: typed result vs typed abstention (Θ) as first-class output
Registry format — single JSON index vs per-agent manifests with a discovery pass
live_band: Secretary right now is an orchestrator alone. Without sub-agents every task is handled at orchestrator-level generality — fine for proposals, too coarse for artisan work (drafting, research, auditing). The fleet is where the framework's jurisdiction contracts become an operational feature rather than a design document. Anchor weight (0.90) is a direction anchor, not a daily-fidelity one.

v1_scope.registry: single index the orchestrator reads to know who's available and what they do
v1_scope.delegation: orchestrator hands a typed task to a sub-agent; sub-agent returns typed result or typed abstention

notes: Declared 2026-04-22 in Jake's scope riff. Comparator hint: system_capability_depth. Direction anchor at 0.90 on the building dimension — doesn't need to score against daily moves. Pairs with secretary.json (this is the next-layer buildout).
